title: Fully Funded African Research Fellowships 2026 by the European Space Agency (ESA): For African Scientists to Work in Italy
summary: The African Research Fellowships Call 2026 under the European Space Agency is one of the most competitive and prestigious research opportunities currently open to African scholars. Designed to empower the next generation of scientists, this fully funded fellowship provides African researchers with the chance to work at the ESA’s ESRIN research center in Italy, collaborate with global experts, and develop cutting-edge solutions using satellite data and advanced technologies. If you are an African researcher seeking international exposure, access to world-class facilities, and the opportunity to contribute to solving Africa’s most pressing challenges, this fellowship could be a life-changing opportunity. About the African Research Fellowship Programme The African Research Fellowship (ARF) is part of the EO AFRICA initiative, a flagship program aimed at strengthening collaboration between Africa and Europe in Earth Observation (EO) science and innovation. Since its launch in 2021, the program has supported several African researchers, enabling them to conduct impactful research and contribute to solving environmental, agricultural, and socio-economic challenges across the continent. Selected fellows will spend one full year at ESA’s ESRIN center, working closely with leading scientists and accessing advanced research infrastructure. Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates) This fellowship is specifically designed for: Eligible Category of People African researchers and scientists Early-career to mid-level researchers Individuals passionate about Earth Observation and satellite data Eligible Countries/Regions All African countries are eligible This means applicants from: East Africa (Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, etc.) West Africa (Nigeria, Ghana, Senegal, etc.) Southern Africa (South Africa, Zambia, Zimbabwe, etc.) North Africa (Egypt, Morocco, Tunisia, etc.) are all encouraged to apply. Academic Qualifications and Requirements To qualify for this fellowship, applicants must meet the following criteria: Minimum Academic Requirements A PhD degree, OR A Master’s degree with substantial research experience Relevant Fields of Study Applicants should have a background in: Earth Observation (EO) Remote sensing Satellite data science Environmental science Geospatial analysis Engineering or related disciplines Additional Requirements Strong research experience and publication record Ability to develop a high-quality research proposal Passion for solving African challenges using technology Research Focus Areas Applicants are required to submit a 1-year research proposal aligned with EO AFRICA priorities. Key areas include: Climate and environmental monitoring Water resource management Food security and agriculture Land use and ecosystem analysis Urban development and sustainability The research must leverage: Satellite Earth Observation data Cloud computing platforms Artificial Intelligence (AI) and advanced modelling techniques Fellowship Benefits This is a fully funded fellowship, offering a wide range of benefits: Financial Benefits Monthly subsistence allowance Travel expenses covered Relocation support package Academic Professional Benefits Work at ESA’s ESRIN research center in Italy Access to high-performance computing tools Collaboration with top European scientists Participation in EO AFRICA Space Academy Career Advancement Exposure to international research networks Opportunity to publish high-impact research Enhanced career prospects in academia and industry What Fellows Will Do Selected fellows will: Conduct innovative research using satellite data Develop solutions for real-world African challenges Collaborate with European and African scientists Contribute to open science and data-sharing initiatives Build tools and insights that inform policy and development The program emphasizes translating scientific data into practical, scalable solutions for Africa. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit a complete application package, including: Research Proposal (max 10 pages using official template) Curriculum Vitae (max 3 pages) Valid Identification (passport preferred) Academic Certificates (PhD or Master’s) Two Signed Reference Letters Failure to follow the required format may result in disqualification. Apply here Why You Should Apply This fellowship is more than just funding—it is a gateway to becoming a leader in Earth Observation science in Africa. Key reasons to apply: Gain international research experience in Europe Work with cutting-edge satellite and AI technologies Contribute to solving Africa’s biggest challenges Join a prestigious network of global scientists Enhance your academic and professional profile Deadline 29th April 2026 The African Research Fellowships Call 2026 represents a rare and highly valuable opportunity for African researchers to step onto the global stage. With full funding, access to world-class facilities, and a strong focus on innovation and impact, this fellowship is ideal for ambitious scientists ready to make a difference. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Fully Funded African Research Fellowships 2026 by the European Space Agency (ESA): For African Scientists to Work in Italy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Frank Jennings Fellowship April 2026: Fully Funded Human Rights Fellowship in Ireland and Geneva for Global Graduates
summary: If you are passionate about human rights advocacy, international law, and global justice, the Frank Jennings Fellowship April 2026 offers one of the most prestigious early-career opportunities in the world. Hosted by Front Line Defenders in partnership with the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR), this fellowship provides hands-on experience working directly with human rights defenders at risk across the globe. What is the Frank Jennings Fellowship? The Frank Jennings Fellowship is a highly competitive international fellowship designed to train and mentor the next generation of human rights professionals. It was established to honor Frank Jennings, a key figure in advancing human rights activism. The program provides a unique combination of NGO and UN-level experience, allowing fellows to work both with an international organization and within the United Nations system. Participants gain deep exposure to: Protection of human rights defenders International advocacy systems UN human rights mechanisms Policy drafting and global reporting processes Fellowship Structure (Detailed Breakdown) The fellowship is divided into three intensive phases, each designed to build expertise progressively: Phase 1: Dublin, Ireland – Training with Front Line Defenders (3–3.5 Months) During this phase, fellows receive foundational training and practical exposure to human rights protection work. Key responsibilities include: Supporting protection coordinators Drafting urgent appeals for at-risk defenders Managing databases and case documentation Conducting research on global human rights issues Phase 2: Geneva, Switzerland – Internship with OHCHR (6 Months) This is the core international experience where fellows work directly within the UN system. Key responsibilities include: Monitoring global human rights violations Drafting communications to governments Contributing to Human Rights Council reports Supporting UN Special Rapporteur mandates Phase 3: Return to Dublin (3 Months) Fellows return to apply their newly acquired UN experience in a practical NGO environment. Activities include: Supporting ongoing human rights cases Contributing to advocacy projects Strengthening field-based protection mechanisms Financial Benefits The fellowship is fully funded with a monthly stipend, making it accessible to candidates worldwide. Dublin: Approximately €1,651/month Geneva: Approximately €1,700/month This financial support helps cover living expenses during the program. Eligibility Criteria This fellowship is specifically designed for early-career professionals and recent graduates with a strong commitment to human rights. Who Can Apply? Recent university graduates (within one year of graduation) Final-year students eligible to graduate before the fellowship begins Individuals with demonstrated interest in human rights work Academic Professional Requirements Applicants must have: Knowledge of international human rights law and the UN system Strong research, writing, and analytical skills Administrative and organizational skills Ability to work in multicultural environments Language Requirements Fluency in English (mandatory) Fluency in Spanish (preferred) or French (often required) Additional Requirements Legal authorization to work in Ireland Strong communication and teamwork abilities Previous experience in human rights organizations (advantageous but not always mandatory Eligible Countries and Regions The fellowship is open to applicants globally, meaning candidates from: Africa Europe Asia North America South America Oceania can apply, provided they meet the eligibility criteria. However, applicants must ensure they meet visa/work authorization requirements, especially for Ireland. Key Skills and Competencies Required To stand out, applicants should demonstrate: Strong policy writing and briefing skills Ability to synthesize complex information Research and analytical thinking Understanding of global human rights frameworks Cultural awareness and adaptability Application Requirements Applicants must typically submit: CV/Resume Cover Letter (explaining motivation and experience) Proof of language proficiency Supporting documents (if required) Make sure your application highlights: Your commitment to human rights Relevant academic or field experience Your interest in international advocacy Apply here Why You Should Apply The Frank Jennings Fellowship is not just a training program—it is a career-launching opportunity that offers: Direct experience with the United Nations Hands-on work with global human rights cases Professional networking with international experts A pathway into careers in NGOs, UN agencies, and international law This fellowship is particularly valuable for those aiming to work in: International organizations Policy and advocacy Human rights law Global development Deadline 14th April 2026 The Frank Jennings Fellowship April 2026 stands out as one of the most impactful fellowships for aspiring human rights professionals worldwide. With its combination of NGO and UN experience, financial support, and global exposure, it provides an unparalleled platform to build a meaningful career in international human rights. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Frank Jennings Fellowship April 2026: Fully Funded Human Rights Fellowship in Ireland and Geneva for Global Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Youth Innovating Democracy 2026 in Strasbourg, France: Fully Funded Council of Europe Youth Event on Shaping the Future of Democracy
summary: The Youth Innovating Democracy 2026 event, organized by the Council of Europe in partnership with the European Youth Forum, is a prestigious, fully funded international youth event designed to empower young leaders to actively contribute to the future of democracy in Europe and beyond. Held at the European Youth Centre in Strasbourg, France from June 24 to June 26, 2026, this unique event is part of the consultation phase of the New Democratic Pact for Europe, an ambitious initiative aimed at reinforcing democratic systems, promoting inclusivity, and addressing emerging challenges such as disinformation, political disengagement, and democratic backsliding. The programme will bring together up to 120 young participants from across Europe and beyond to engage in meaningful dialogue, develop innovative solutions, and directly influence policy recommendations that will shape the future of democracy. About the Youth Innovating Democracy Event The Youth Innovating Democracy event is a flagship youth engagement initiative under the New Democratic Pact for Europe. It is designed as a platform for young people to: Share their perspectives on democracy and governance Propose innovative solutions to strengthen democratic systems Engage directly with policymakers and political leaders Contribute to shaping future democratic frameworks This event reflects the Council of Europe’s long-standing commitment to youth participation in democratic decision-making processes. Why This Event Matters Across Europe and globally, democracy faces increasing challenges, including: Democratic backsliding Disinformation and misinformation Declining trust in institutions Youth underrepresentation in politics The New Democratic Pact for Europe aims to address these issues by strengthening democratic foundations, amplifying citizen participation, and innovating governance systems. The Youth Event plays a critical role in this process by ensuring that young people are at the center of these conversations and solutions. Key Themes of the Event The event is structured around three major thematic areas that address pressing challenges in democracy. 1. Juvenate Politics: Innovating the System This theme focuses on increasing youth participation in political systems. Key discussions include: Youth representation in decision-making Electoral reforms (e.g., youth quotas, voting age) Civic education and political engagement Inclusion of young people in governance 2. Live Equality: The Promise of Democracy This theme addresses social and economic inequalities affecting young people. Key focus areas: Access to social rights Economic justice and inclusion Impact of crises on youth (economic, political, health) Addressing marginalization and exclusion 3. Empower Communities: Building Participation Ecosystems This theme explores how to create enabling environments for youth participation. Topics include: Role of youth organizations and civil society Participatory democracy models Community engagement and grassroots initiatives Protection of democratic freedoms Objectives of the Programme The event aims to: Engage youth in shaping the New Democratic Pact for Europe Highlight innovative practices in youth participation Foster dialogue between youth and policymakers Promote democratic values and human rights Build a network of young leaders Programme Structure The three-day programme includes: Plenary sessions with political leaders Interactive workshops and discussions Creative and participatory activities Networking opportunities Development of policy proposals and outputs Participants will contribute directly to recommendations and creative outputs that will inform the New Democratic Pact. Participant Profile The event seeks a diverse group of young people, including: Representatives of international youth organizations Members of national or local youth councils Youth activists in democracy and human rights Participants from youth movements and initiatives Youth delegates and policy contributors Young artists, journalists, and content creators Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Aged between 18 and 35 years Resident in a country party to the European Cultural Convention (exceptions possible) Able to work in English or French Available for the full duration of the event Able to contribute to follow-up actions after the event Selection Process Up to 120 participants will be selected Selection ensures diversity in: Gender Geographic representation Experience levels Cultural backgrounds A waiting list will also be established. Financial Support and Benefits The event is fully funded by the Council of Europe. Covered Costs Travel expenses (according to Council of Europe rules) Accommodation (European Youth Centre or nearby hotel) Meals during the event Participants are expected to arrive on June 23 and depart on June 27, 2026. Working Languages English French Participants must be able to communicate effectively in at least one of these languages. Learn more HERE. Why You Should Apply This event offers a unique opportunity to: Engage directly with European policymakers Influence the future of democracy Build international networks Develop leadership and advocacy skills Gain global exposure Application Process Applicants must: Complete the Official Online Application Form Submit before the deadline Applications must be submitted through the official platform; incomplete or late applications will not be considered. Application Deadline Deadline: April 19, 2026 (23:59 CET) Please read the call for applications for more information. Discover more global Conference opportunities on OFY. The post Youth Innovating Democracy 2026 in Strasbourg, France: Fully Funded Council of Europe Youth Event on Shaping the Future of Democracy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: West Africa Democracy Fund 2026 by TrustAfrica: Grants up to $500,000 for Civil Society, Youth, and Governance Innovation Projects
summary: The West Africa Democracy Fund (WADF) 2026, launched by TrustAfrica, is a transformative funding opportunity aimed at strengthening democratic governance, civic participation, and institutional accountability across West Africa. This major grant initiative supports organizations working at local, national, and regional levels to reimagine governance systems and amplify citizens’ voices in shaping their political and social futures. Backed by leading global philanthropic organizations—including the Ford Foundation, MacArthur Foundation, Luminate Group, Open Society Foundations, and Carnegie Corporation of New York—the WADF represents a significant investment in advancing democracy, inclusion, and resilience in the region. With grants ranging from $50,000 to $500,000, this programme offers a powerful opportunity for civil society organizations, youth groups, and institutions to implement impactful, innovative, and scalable governance solutions. About the West Africa Democracy Fund (WADF) The West Africa Democracy Fund was officially launched in Abuja, Nigeria, as a response to the evolving democratic challenges across the region. The fund seeks to support initiatives that: Strengthen democratic institutions Promote inclusive governance Enhance citizen engagement Foster accountability and transparency At its core, WADF is about rethinking and redefining the role of citizens in governance systems across West Africa. Programme Vision and Purpose The WADF aims to: Expand democratic and civic spaces Strengthen social contracts between governments and citizens Promote inclusive political participation Support resilient and accountable governance systems The fund emphasizes the importance of citizen voices in shaping governance outcomes and future political systems. Key Objectives of the Fund The WADF focuses on several strategic objectives that guide funded projects: Incorporating Citizens’ Perspectives Integrate citizen aspirations into public policy and development agendas Strengthening Social Contracts Promote frameworks that guide elections, media use, and governance systems Mobilizing Communities Engage youth, religious groups, media, trade unions, and civil society Fostering Regional Collaboration Encourage partnerships across countries and institutions Strengthening Public Institutions Support institutions like the judiciary, electoral bodies, and media regulators Thematic Areas Applicants must align their proposals with one or more of the following thematic areas: 1. Multi-Stakeholder Dialogues Projects may include: Public consultations and town hall meetings Community dialogue platforms Cultural and religious engagement spaces Policy discussions and social cohesion initiatives Expected outcomes: Policy recommendations Community-driven governance solutions Strengthened citizen-government relationships 2. Advocacy for Institutional Reform Eligible activities include: Advocacy for policy and governance reforms Strengthening participation of marginalized groups Engagement with regional institutions such as ECOWAS Promoting democratic frameworks and governance protocols 3. Strengthening Strategic Public Institutions Projects may focus on: Judicial systems Electoral management bodies Media and telecommunications regulators Support may include: Capacity building Technical assistance Institutional reform strategies 4. Innovative Projects The fund encourages creative and forward-thinking ideas that: Reimagine democracy Introduce new governance models Address emerging challenges in the region Grant Categories Applicants can apply under different funding levels: Small Grants: Minimum $50,000 Medium Grants: Up to $250,000 Large Grants: Up to $500,000 Project Duration 12 to 24 months Application Formats Single organization Consortium Network-based applications Eligibility Criteria Organizations must meet the following requirements: Geographic Eligibility Applicants must be: Based in or operating within: Burkina Faso Ghana Mali Niger Nigeria Senegal Togo Regional organizations working across these countries are also eligible. Organizational Requirements Applicants must: Be legally registered organizations Have experience in: Governance Democracy Citizen engagement Political participation Demonstrate: Financial management capacity Experience managing similar grant sizes Consortium Applications Lead organization must have prior experience managing similar projects or regranting What the Fund Does Not Support The WADF does not fund: General institutional support (non-project-based funding) Election monitoring activities (except specific use cases) Partisan political activities Lobbying for specific legislation Organizations on restricted sanction lists Why You Should Apply The West Africa Democracy Fund offers: Significant funding for impactful projects Opportunity to influence governance systems Support for innovative and scalable solutions Regional and global recognition Collaboration opportunities across West Africa Application Process Applicants must complete the application through the Official Platform. Required Documents Completed application form Organizational and project budgets Legal registration certificate Audited financial statements (if applicable) CVs of key staff (maximum of five) Submission Guidelines Applications must be submitted via the Fluxx platform Submissions can be in English or French Email or paper applications will not be accepted Application Timeline Application Deadline: April 30, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit early to avoid last-minute challenges. Additional Opportunities Applicants can also: Participate in an information webinar (April 13, 2026) Contact the project manager for support Click HERE to learn more about the West Africa Democracy Fund (WADF) 2026. Visit Opportunities for youth for more global Grant opportunities. title: Digital Civics Academy 2026: Empowering the Next Generation of Ethical Tech Leaders
summary: The rapid rise of artificial intelligence and digital platforms has created unprecedented opportunities—and challenges—for young people around the world. The Digital Civics Academy (DCA) 2026 is stepping up to equip the next generation with the tools, confidence, and leadership skills needed to shape a healthier digital future. This transformative one-week summer program is designed specifically for teens who are passionate about advocacy, technology, and creating meaningful change in their communities. What Is the Digital Civics Academy? The Digital Civics Academy is an immersive, youth-centered masterclass co-designed by young people, for young people . It focuses on empowering participants to become ethical leaders in technology and digital wellbeing. The 2026 theme, “Agency in the Age of Artificial Intelligence,” explores how youth can responsibly navigate emerging technologies while protecting their mental health and digital wellbeing. Participants will gain hands-on experience in activism, storytelling, and leadership—key skills needed to influence both local and global conversations around technology. Program Dates and Format Program Duration: July 20–24, 2026 Format: Primarily virtual Optional In-Person Opportunities: New York City San Francisco Washington, DC Los Angeles The hybrid model ensures accessibility while still offering unique real-world exposure for those who can attend in person. Application Deadlines Early Decision Deadline: April 15, 2026 (11:59 PM PDT) Decision released by the end of April Regular Decision Deadline: April 30, 2026 (11:59 PM PDT) Decision released mid to late May Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early due to limited capacity. What You Will Learn Participants of the Digital Civics Academy will develop both technical and interpersonal skills essential for leadership in the digital age. Core Learning Areas: Advocacy and civic engagement Multimedia storytelling Leadership and collaboration Policy and legislation translation Digital wellbeing and self-advocacy Students will also engage directly with policymakers, tech designers, and industry leaders, gaining insights into how decisions are made and how they can influence them. Real-World Exposure and Opportunities Past cohorts have had the opportunity to: Visit major tech companies like Pinterest and Snapchat Speak with legislators at the California State Capitol Learn from influential leaders, including policymakers and media professionals These experiences provide participants with a rare behind-the-scenes look at how technology, policy, and advocacy intersect. Mentorship and Expert Guidance The program is facilitated by trained team members and industry experts who lead workshops and masterclasses. Participants will learn directly from professionals shaping the future of AI, policy, and digital wellbeing. Cost and Accessibility One of the most compelling aspects of the Digital Civics Academy is that it is completely free of charge . Thanks to generous sponsorships, students from diverse backgrounds can participate without financial barriers. Who Should Apply? The program is ideal for young individuals who: Are passionate about technology and social impact Want to develop leadership and advocacy skills Are eager to collaborate with like-minded peers Have a desire to make meaningful change No prior experience in advocacy or technology is required, making this an inclusive opportunity for beginners and experienced changemakers alike. Why You Should Apply The Digital Civics Academy is more than just a summer program—it is a launchpad for young leaders who want to shape the future of technology responsibly. With access to expert mentorship, real-world experiences, and a global network of peers, participants leave the program equipped to drive meaningful change in their communities and beyond. If you are ready to become a voice for ethical technology and digital wellbeing, this is your opportunity to step forward. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more summer programs opportunities, click here . The post Digital Civics Academy 2026: Empowering the Next Generation of Ethical Tech Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Cameron Impact Scholarship 2026 (Class of 2027): Fully Funded Undergraduate Scholarship in the USA
summary: The Cameron Impact Scholarship 2026 is a highly competitive, fully funded undergraduate scholarship designed for exceptional high school students who demonstrate leadership, academic excellence, and a deep commitment to creating positive change in their communities and the world. Each year, approximately 15 students are selected to receive this prestigious four-year scholarship, which supports their academic journey at any accredited U.S. college or university. What the Cameron Impact Scholarship Covers This scholarship provides comprehensive financial support, including: Full tuition All mandatory fees Books and academic materials Recipients have the flexibility to attend any accredited four-year college or university in the United States , making it one of the most versatile fully funded scholarships available. Who Should Apply? The Cameron Impact Scholarship is looking for students who go beyond academic success. Ideal applicants are: Visionary leaders with a strong sense of purpose Actively involved in community service or civic engagement Passionate about making a measurable impact Committed to long-term contributions toward the greater good This program values impact-driven individuals who aspire to become future change-makers. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Minimum GPA of 3.7 (unweighted) or equivalent Must be a U.S. citizen Member of the high school graduating Class of 2027 Planning to enroll full-time in a four-year U.S. college or university Demonstrated excellence in leadership, extracurricular activities, and community service Application Requirements To apply, candidates must submit: Completed online application Two letters of recommendation (one from school personnel) Academic transcript (unofficial copies accepted) Incomplete applications will not be reviewed, and no extensions are granted. Step-by-Step Application Process To successfully apply for the Cameron Impact Scholarship, follow these steps carefully: Fill out the Applicant Profile form on the official website Create a username and password Activate your account via email (check spam folder if needed) Log in through the “Applicant Login” portal Complete the application and essays (pay attention to word limits) Upload your transcript and two recommendation letters Alternatively, documents can be emailed to: admin@bryancameronef.org Submit your application Important Tips: The system does not allow saving and returning later , so prepare your answers in advance (e.g., in Word) Disable browser cookies to avoid technical errors Ensure all required documents are submitted— incomplete applications will not be considered Selection Process The selection process includes: Rolling application review Selection of approximately 100 finalists In-person interviews in Fall 2026 Final selection and announcement in December 2026 Recipients are also required to attend an annual awards ceremony during the scholarship period. Equity in a Merit-Based Program The Cameron Impact Scholarship evaluates applicants through an equity-focused lens, considering: Individual circumstances and available opportunities Challenges faced and overcome Impact achieved relative to resources This ensures that excellence is recognized across diverse backgrounds and experiences. Application Deadline May 1, 2026, at 12:00 PM PT (Noon) Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis and will close once 3,000 completed and eligible applications are received or by the deadline—whichever comes first. Early submission is strongly recommended. Why You Should Apply This scholarship is more than financial support—it is an investment in future leaders. Benefits include: Full financial coverage for your undergraduate degree Access to a powerful network of changemakers Opportunities for collaboration and global impact APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts The Cameron Impact Scholarship 2026 is a life-changing opportunity for high school students who are ready to lead, serve, and create meaningful change. With limited slots and a rolling application process, applying early can significantly improve your chances. Prepare thoroughly, submit early, and take a bold step toward becoming a global leader. title: Anne van Biema Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Japanese Visual Arts at the Smithsonian
summary: The Anne van Biema Fellowship 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for advanced scholars to conduct in-depth research on Japanese visual arts at one of the world’s most prestigious institutions—the Smithsonian’s National Museum of Asian Art in Washington, DC. This highly competitive fellowship is designed to support innovative and impactful research that contributes to both academic scholarship and public understanding of Japanese art. With generous funding, access to world-class collections, and an intellectually stimulating environment, this program is ideal for researchers seeking to elevate their work to an international level. About the Anne van Biema Fellowship The Anne van Biema Fellowship was established through a bequest with the goal of promoting excellence in research and publication in Japanese visual arts . It supports scholars working on projects that demonstrate: Strong academic merit Originality and innovation Clear and rigorous methodology Potential for significant scholarly publication The fellowship welcomes interdisciplinary research , provided that the primary focus remains on Japanese visual arts. Host Institution: Smithsonian’s National Museum of Asian Art Fellows will conduct their research at the Smithsonian’s National Museum of Asian Art, which houses one of the most important collections of Asian art in the world. Participants benefit from: Access to extensive art collections and archives Use of Smithsonian libraries and research facilities A collaborative academic environment with leading scholars This setting provides an ideal platform for producing high-quality research with global impact. Fellowship Duration and Structure For the 2026 cycle, the fellowship duration is: 3 to 9 months Appointments begin between August 1 and December 1, 2026 Fellows are not required to reside in Washington, DC for the entire duration, offering flexibility while maintaining access to institutional resources. Funding and Benefits The Anne van Biema Fellowship provides comprehensive financial and institutional support: Stipend Up to $45,000 for 3–9 months Approximately $5,000 per month , prorated based on duration Additional Research Support Up to $5,000 for research and travel expenses Institutional Resources Dedicated research space Access to a networked computer Full access to collections, archives, and libraries Academic Expectations Fellows are expected to: Dedicate full-time effort to their research project Actively participate in the museum’s scholarly community Engage in academic discussions and programs Present a research-in-progress seminar during their fellowship This ensures both intellectual exchange and professional visibility within the academic community. Eligibility Criteria The fellowship is open to: Postdoctoral and senior scholars Researchers with a strong background in: Japanese visual arts Art history Related interdisciplinary fields Nationality Open to all nationalities worldwide Visa Information Applicants are responsible for meeting U.S. visa requirements. If necessary, the Smithsonian may support eligible candidates through the J-1 Visa Exchange Visitor Program . Application Requirements Applicants must submit a complete application package in English, including: Application cover sheet Curriculum Vitae (CV) with full publication list Research proposal: 250-word précis Maximum 2,000-word narrative Budget plan (maximum $5,000 for research expenses) Two letters of recommendation All application materials must be submitted via email. Application Deadline and Timeline Application Deadline: May 4, 2026 Notification of Results: By the end of June 2026 Fellowship Start Period: Between August 1 and December 1, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure all materials, including recommendation letters, are submitted on time. Why You Should Apply The Anne van Biema Fellowship is more than just funding—it is a gateway to academic excellence and international recognition. It offers scholars the chance to: Conduct research in a world-renowned institution Access rare and valuable art collections and archives Produce impactful publications in Japanese art history Collaborate with leading experts in the field Strengthen their academic and professional reputation For scholars dedicated to advancing the study of Japanese visual arts, this fellowship represents a prestigious and transformative opportunity. title: African Food Research Acceleration Program (AFRAP) 2026: Fully-Funded Virtual Fellowship for Emerging African Food Scientists
summary: The African Food Research Acceleration Program (AFRAP) Cohort II is now open for applications, offering a unique opportunity for early-career food scientists across Africa to gain hands-on research experience, mentorship, and publication support — all at no cost. Designed by the Centre for African Food Research and Innovation (CAFRI) , AFRAP is a six-month, fully virtual fellowship that equips participants with the practical skills needed to design, execute, and publish original research focused on Africa’s food systems. What is AFRAP? The African Food Research Acceleration Program (AFRAP) is a structured, mentorship-led fellowship aimed at building the next generation of African food researchers. It focuses on empowering participants with real-world research skills while addressing critical challenges in African food systems — from production to consumption. Participants work closely with experienced mentors, attend expert-led workshops, and develop a publishable research manuscript as their main output. Programme Highlights Duration: 6 months Format: Fully virtual Cost: Free (no application fee) Mentorship: 1:1 mentor matching Outcome: One publishable research manuscript Network: Pan-African cohort of researchers What You Will Gain 1. Research Methodology Design Skills Learn how to formulate research questions, design studies, and develop strong research proposals. 2. Expert Mentorship Work closely with experienced researchers who guide you throughout the entire research process. 3. Data Analysis Expertise Gain hands-on experience with both quantitative and qualitative data analysis tools. 4. Publication-Ready Manuscript Graduate with a research paper ready for submission to peer-reviewed journals. 5. Pan-African Professional Network Collaborate with peers and build lasting relationships with researchers across the continent. Programme Structure The AFRAP fellowship is built around four key pillars: Research Design Develop strong research questions, objectives, and methodologies. Literature Mastery Conduct comprehensive literature reviews and identify research gaps. Data Analysis Learn data collection methods and analysis techniques. Manuscript Writing Structure and finalize a scientific paper for publication. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a citizen or resident of any African country Be a final-year undergraduate, master’s student, or early-career professional in a food-related field Demonstrate a strong interest in food science, nutrition, food safety, or food systems Be able to commit 4–6 hours per week for six months Have access to a computer and reliable internet connection Note: No prior research experience is required. Application Process To apply for AFRAP Cohort II, follow these steps: Review the eligibility criteria Prepare your CV or LinkedIn profile Complete the online application form Write three short motivation essays Submit your application Participate in an interview (if shortlisted) Receive onboarding materials upon acceptance APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Deadline Application Deadline: 30 June 2026 Early application is strongly encouraged as the selection process is competitive. Final Thoughts The AFRAP fellowship is more than just a training program — it is a launchpad for African food researchers to build credible research profiles, publish impactful work, and contribute to solving real challenges in Africa’s food systems. If you are passionate about food research and ready to develop your skills under expert guidance, this program offers a powerful opportunity to grow and make a meaningful impact. title: ifa Cultural Heritage Scholarship 2026 in Germany: Fully Funded Programme for Curators, Culture Managers, and Heritage Professionals
summary: The ifa (Institut für Auslandsbeziehungen) Cultural Heritage Scholarship 2026, supported by the Alexander Rave Foundation, is a prestigious fully funded international programme designed for cultural practitioners from developing countries to advance their expertise in cultural heritage management, museum transformation, and transcultural dialogue. This highly competitive scholarship offers participants the opportunity to undertake a 3 to 6-month professional stay in Germany, working closely with host institutions such as museums, galleries, and cultural research centers. The programme is rooted in fostering global collaboration, strengthening democratic values, and promoting intercultural understanding through heritage, archives, and collections. With a strong emphasis on sustainability, inclusion, and decolonial practices, this scholarship represents a transformative opportunity for professionals seeking to contribute to the evolution of cultural institutions worldwide. About the ifa Cultural Heritage Scholarship The ifa Cultural Heritage Scholarship is part of ifa’s broader mission to promote international cultural exchange, dialogue, and cooperation. Through this programme, cultural professionals engage in transnational learning, knowledge exchange, and collaborative project development. The scholarship is specifically designed to: Strengthen global networks in cultural heritage Encourage interdisciplinary collaboration Promote freedom of expression and academic discourse Foster democratic and inclusive cultural spaces Programme Overview Key Details Host Country: Germany Duration: 3 to 6 months Optional Reciprocal Stay: 2 to 12 weeks (Germany to DAC country) Application Deadline: June 30, 2026 Earliest Start Date: February 1, 2027 Programme End Date: December 31, 2027 Applicants will be notified of results by September 2026. Thematic Focus Areas The scholarship focuses on the transformation of museums and exhibition spaces in line with the International Council of Museums (ICOM) definition (2023). Core Themes Cultural participation Sustainability in cultural institutions Non-discriminatory and inclusive spaces Decolonial approaches to cultural heritage These themes reflect contemporary global discussions on the role of culture in shaping equitable and inclusive societies. Who Can Apply The programme is open to professionals working in cultural and heritage sectors. Eligible Applicants Include Curators Restorers Cultural mediators Culture managers Applicants must be actively engaged in work related to: Cultural heritage Collections Archive records Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the scholarship, applicants must meet the following requirements: Academic and Professional Requirements Completed higher education or vocational training Demonstrated professional experience in cultural institutions Strong involvement in heritage, collections, or archives Language Requirements Very good command of English Knowledge of German or the host country’s language is an advantage but not mandatory Additional Requirements Must secure a non-commercial host institution Must submit a jointly developed project proposal with the host institution Must not hold another scholarship during the funding period Host Institution Requirements Host institutions play a critical role in the success of the programme. Host Institutions Must: Be non-profit organizations Be museums, galleries, or cultural/research institutes Have a physical location Possess a collection or archive Assign a dedicated contact person Collaborate with the applicant on a project proposal Scholarship Benefits The ifa Cultural Heritage Scholarship offers comprehensive financial and logistical support. Financial Benefits Monthly stipend of EUR 1,500 Family allowance: EUR 250 for spouse EUR 250 per child Additional Support Travel costs (round trip) Visa costs Health insurance (including family coverage) Monthly public transport pass Up to EUR 500 for language courses Programme Structure Participants will: Work on a project related to collections or institutional transformation Engage in knowledge exchange with host institutions Contribute to sustainable cultural practices Participate in professional and academic discussions At the end of the programme, fellows must submit a final report documenting their work and learning outcomes. Application Process The application process is structured and requires careful preparation. Key Steps Identify a suitable host institution Develop a joint project proposal Prepare all required documents Submit the application via the official online form Click HERE to apply Important Notes Applications must be complete before submission Temporary saving of applications is not possible Applications submitted via email will not be accepted Late or incomplete applications will be rejected Selection and Award Details Maximum of 10 scholarships awarded annually Selection is highly competitive Projects must demonstrate strong relevance and impact Inclusivity and Diversity The programme actively encourages applications from: Women Black individuals and people of color LGBTQIA+ individuals Persons with disabilities Individuals from historically marginalized or colonized contexts This reflects the programme’s commitment to equity, diversity, and inclusive cultural development. Why You Should Apply The ifa Cultural Heritage Scholarship provides a unique opportunity to: Gain international experience in Germany Work with leading cultural institutions Expand professional networks Develop innovative approaches to cultural heritage Contribute to global conversations on inclusion and sustainability Learn more about the ifa (Institut für Auslandsbeziehungen) Cultural Heritage Scholarship 2026 HERE. Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE. title: Call for Grant Proposals: Strengthening CSO Capacities in the Maldives
summary: The Global Environment Facility Small Grants Programme (GEF SGP) in the Maldives has announced a Capacity Development Grant for Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) , community-based organizations (CBOs), and research institutions. With a grant of up to US$75,000 and applications open until 18 April 2026 , this initiative aims to strengthen the technical skills, proposal development, and monitoring and evaluation (M E) capacities of local organizations. By equipping Maldivian CSOs with the tools to design high-quality proposals and manage projects effectively, this grant promotes sustainable development, community wellbeing, and environmental stewardship. Objectives of the Grant The Capacity Development Grant is part of the eighth operational phase of the SGP (OP8), which builds on over 30 years of empowering local civil society. The grant focuses on three key areas: Grant Proposal Writing and Technical Skills Selected organizations will conduct workshops and training sessions to improve the ability of CSOs and CBOs to write compelling and fundable grant proposals. These sessions will include guidance on donor expectations, structuring proposals, common pitfalls, and practical exercises. One dedicated workshop will focus on helping existing SGP grantees with interim and final report submissions, strengthening their compliance with funding requirements. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Knowledge Management Organizations will support grantees in monitoring and documenting project outcomes, using simplified M E tools, ensuring data quality, and developing reporting frameworks. This component aims to enhance grantees’ ability to track progress, analyze results, and disseminate lessons learned effectively, allowing successful projects to be scaled and replicated. Advocacy and Communication The grant also includes conducting a two-year advocacy campaign to raise awareness of the work and successes of Maldivian CSOs. This involves producing communication materials such as social media content, infographics, short videos, and reports, as well as organizing webinars, virtual networks, and roundtable meetings with stakeholders. Through these activities, CSOs can amplify their impact, influence policy, and foster multi-stakeholder collaboration. Why This Opportunity Is Important Local civil society organizations are vital actors in addressing social, environmental, and economic challenges in the Maldives. However, many face limitations in accessing funding, managing projects, and effectively communicating their results. By enhancing capacity in proposal writing, M E, and advocacy, this grant equips CSOs with the skills necessary to secure resources, manage projects efficiently, and generate meaningful impact in their communities. Moreover, the grant encourages the sharing of knowledge and best practices, promoting innovation and replication of successful projects. By linking CSOs, CBOs, local councils, line ministries, and development partners, the initiative strengthens networks and fosters collaboration. This ultimately leads to more sustainable community-driven development initiatives, improved environmental resilience, and empowerment of marginalized groups, including women, youth, and persons with disabilities. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to: National and local CSOs and NGOs Community-based organizations (CBOs) Research bodies with relevant experience Eligible organizations must demonstrate experience in capacity building, proposal development, M E support, and advocacy campaigns. The team should include members with social sciences or management backgrounds, expertise in communications, and hands-on experience with community development projects. How to Apply Interested organizations should submit: A detailed proposal outlining methodology and approach for all three components (workshops, M E support, advocacy). A budget and work plan covering a period of 24–36 months (2026–2028). CVs of proposed team members with clearly defined roles. A letter of application describing relevant experience and capacity. All documents must be submitted as attachments; externally hosted or editable links will not be accepted. Applications will be evaluated solely on materials received by the deadline. Apply by: 18 April 2026 | 23:00 HRS (MVT) For more information and submission details, visit the official call for proposals . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.
